intro_message: Resumen automático del ecosistema de IA.

items.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
items.summary: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”

items.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on

items.title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
items.summary: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending. OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building their way out of single-supplier risk.
